Defines and manages memory protection unit (MPU) regions for the Pebble firmware, including setup and access functions.

# Purpose
The code defines a module for managing memory protection unit (MPU) regions in a system. It includes functions and data structures to configure and query different memory regions, such as flash memory, read-only sections, and stack guards. The module provides a set of predefined `MpuRegion` structures, each representing a specific memory region with attributes like base address, size, cache policy, and access permissions for privileged and unprivileged users. These regions include `s_readonly_bss_region`, `s_isr_stack_guard_region`, `s_app_region`, and others, each serving a specific purpose in the memory layout.

The function [`memory_layout_dump_mpu_regions_to_dbgserial`](<#memory_layout_dump_mpu_regions_to_dbgserial>) logs the configuration of each MPU region, including whether it is enabled, its address, size, and access permissions. The [`memory_layout_setup_mpu`](<#memory_layout_setup_mpu>) function configures the MPU by setting up the defined regions and enabling the MPU. The module also provides functions to retrieve specific `MpuRegion` structures, such as [`memory_layout_get_app_region`](<#memory_layout_get_app_region>) and [`memory_layout_get_worker_region`](<#memory_layout_get_worker_region>). Additionally, it includes utility functions to check if a pointer, buffer, or C-string is within a specified memory region, ensuring that memory accesses are within the defined boundaries. This module is intended to be part of a larger system, providing essential memory protection and access control functionality.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `memory_layout.h`
- `kernel/logging_private.h`
- `system/passert.h`
- `util/math.h`
- `util/size.h`
- `util/string.h`
- `kernel/mpu_regions.auto.h`
- `inttypes.h`
- `string.h`


# Global Variables

---
### MEMORY\_REGION\_NAMES
- **Type**: ``static const char* const[]``
- **Description**: An array of constant character pointers that holds the names of different memory regions. The array contains 8 elements, each representing a specific memory region name.
- **Use**: Used to map memory region indices to their corresponding names for logging and debugging purposes.


---
### \_\_privileged\_functions\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t[]``
- **Description**: Represents the start address of the privileged functions section in memory. This variable is declared as an external constant array of unsigned 32-bit integers.
- **Use**: Used to reference the beginning of the memory region where privileged functions are stored.


---
### \_\_privileged\_functions\_size\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t[]``
- **Description**: Represents the size of the privileged functions section in memory. It is declared as an external constant array of unsigned 32-bit integers.
- **Use**: Used to define the size of the memory region allocated for privileged functions.


---
### \_\_unpriv\_ro\_bss\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t[]``
- **Description**: Represents the start address of the unprivileged read-only BSS (Block Started by Symbol) section in memory. This section is part of the memory layout that is accessible to unprivileged applications for read-only operations.
- **Use**: Used to define the base address of the unprivileged read-only BSS region in the memory protection unit (MPU) configuration.


---
### \_\_unpriv\_ro\_bss\_size\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t[]``
- **Description**: Represents the size of the unprivileged read-only BSS (Block Started by Symbol) section in memory. This section is part of the memory layout that is accessible to unprivileged applications for read-only purposes.
- **Use**: Used to define the size of the unprivileged read-only BSS region in the memory protection unit (MPU) configuration.


---
### \_\_isr\_stack\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t[]``
- **Description**: Represents the start address of the Interrupt Service Routine (ISR) stack. It is declared as an external constant array of unsigned 32-bit integers.
- **Use**: Used to define the base address for the ISR stack guard region in the memory layout.


---
### \_\_stack\_guard\_size\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t``
- **Description**: Represents the size of the stack guard region in memory. It is used to define the size of memory protection regions for stack guards.
- **Use**: Used to set the size of stack guard regions in memory protection unit (MPU) configurations.


---
### \_\_APP\_RAM\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t[]``
- **Description**: Represents a constant array of unsigned 32-bit integers that is used to define a memory region for application RAM. It is declared as an external variable, indicating that its definition is provided elsewhere in the program.
- **Use**: Used to specify the base address for the application stack guard region in the memory layout.


---
### \_\_WORKER\_RAM\_\_
- **Type**: ``uint32_t[]``
- **Description**: Represents a constant array of unsigned 32-bit integers that is used to define a memory region for worker tasks. The array is declared as an external variable, indicating that its definition is located in another file.
- **Use**: Used to specify the base address for the worker stack guard region in the memory layout.


---
### \_\_FLASH\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t[]``
- **Description**: Represents the starting address of the flash memory region in the system. It is declared as an external constant array of unsigned 32-bit integers.
- **Use**: Used to define the base address for the flash memory region in the memory protection unit (MPU) configuration.


---
### \_\_FLASH\_size\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t[]``
- **Description**: Represents the size of the flash memory region in the system. It is declared as an external constant array of unsigned 32-bit integers.
- **Use**: Used to define the size of the flash memory region in the `s_microflash_region` structure for memory protection unit (MPU) configuration.


---
### \_\_kernel\_main\_stack\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t[]``
- **Description**: Represents the start address of the main stack for the kernel. It is declared as an external constant array of unsigned 32-bit integers.
- **Use**: Used to define the base address for the kernel's main stack in memory layout configurations.


---
### \_\_kernel\_bg\_stack\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t[]``
- **Description**: Represents the start address of the kernel background stack in memory. It is declared as an external constant array of unsigned 32-bit integers.
- **Use**: Used to define the base address for the kernel background stack guard region in the memory protection unit (MPU) setup.


---
### s\_readonly\_bss\_region
- **Type**: ``MpuRegion``
- **Description**: Defines a memory protection unit (MPU) region for the read-only BSS section. This region is configured to be enabled, with a base address and size defined by `__unpriv_ro_bss_start__` and `__unpriv_ro_bss_size__`, respectively. It allows privileged read and write access, user read access, but no user write access.
- **Use**: Used to configure the MPU to protect the read-only BSS section in memory.


---
### s\_isr\_stack\_guard\_region
- **Type**: ``MpuRegion``
- **Description**: Defines a memory protection unit (MPU) region for the ISR stack guard. It specifies the region number, base address, size, cache policy, and access permissions for both privileged and user modes.
- **Use**: Used to configure the MPU to protect the ISR stack from unauthorized access.


---
### s\_app\_stack\_guard\_region
- **Type**: ``MpuRegion``
- **Description**: Defines a memory protection unit (MPU) region for the application stack guard. It specifies the region number, base address, size, cache policy, and access permissions for both privileged and user modes.
- **Use**: Used to configure the MPU to protect the application stack by preventing read and write access.


---
### s\_worker\_stack\_guard\_region
- **Type**: ``MpuRegion``
- **Description**: Defines a memory protection unit (MPU) region for the worker stack guard. It specifies the region number, base address, size, cache policy, and access permissions for both privileged and user modes.
- **Use**: Used to configure the MPU to protect the worker stack by preventing read and write access.


---
### s\_app\_region
- **Type**: ``MpuRegion``
- **Description**: Defines a memory protection unit (MPU) region for application RAM. It specifies the region number, base address, size, cache policy, and access permissions for privileged operations.
- **Use**: Used to configure the MPU for application RAM with specific access permissions and cache policy.


---
### s\_worker\_region
- **Type**: ``MpuRegion``
- **Description**: Defines a memory protection unit (MPU) region for worker RAM. It specifies the region number, base address, size, cache policy, and access permissions for privileged operations.
- **Use**: Used to configure the MPU for the worker RAM region with specific access permissions and cache policies.


---
### s\_microflash\_region
- **Type**: ``MpuRegion``
- **Description**: Defines a memory protection unit (MPU) region for the microflash memory. It specifies the region number, base address, size, cache policy, and access permissions for both privileged and user modes.
- **Use**: Used to configure the MPU for the microflash memory region, allowing read access for both privileged and user modes, but write access only for privileged mode.


---
### s\_kernel\_main\_stack\_guard\_region
- **Type**: ``MpuRegion``
- **Description**: Defines a memory protection unit (MPU) region for the kernel's main stack guard. It specifies the region number, base address, size, cache policy, and access permissions for both privileged and user modes.
- **Use**: Used to configure the MPU to protect the kernel's main stack from unauthorized access.


---
### s\_kernel\_bg\_stack\_guard\_region
- **Type**: ``MpuRegion``
- **Description**: Defines a memory protection unit (MPU) region for the kernel background stack guard. It specifies the region number, base address, size, cache policy, and access permissions for privileged and user modes.
- **Use**: Used to configure the MPU to protect the kernel background stack from unauthorized access.


# Functions

---
### memory\_layout\_dump\_mpu\_regions\_to\_dbgserial<!-- {{#callable:memory_layout_dump_mpu_regions_to_dbgserial}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/kernel/memory_layout.c#L43>)

Logs the configuration of MPU regions to the debug serial output.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize a buffer of 90 characters for logging purposes.
    - Iterate over a fixed number of MPU regions (`NUM_REGIONS` is 8).
    - For each region, retrieve its configuration using `mpu_get_region(i)`.
    - Check if the region is enabled; if not, log that it is not enabled and continue to the next region.
    - If the region is enabled, log its index, name, base address, size, and access permissions (privileged and user read/write).
    - If the region has disabled subregions, log the disabled subregions.
- **Output**: No return value; outputs log messages to the debug serial.


---
### memory\_layout\_setup\_mpu<!-- {{#callable:memory_layout_setup_mpu}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/kernel/memory_layout.c#L213>)

Configures the Memory Protection Unit (MPU) by setting up specific memory regions and enabling the MPU.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `mpu_set_region` with `s_microflash_region` to set up the flash memory region for unprivileged read access.
    - Calls `mpu_set_region` with `s_readonly_bss_region` to set up a read-only RAM region for unprivileged access.
    - Calls `mpu_set_region` with `s_isr_stack_guard_region` to set up an ISR stack guard region.
    - Calls `mpu_enable` to enable the MPU after setting up the regions.
- **Output**: No output is returned as the function is of type `void`.

### memory\_layout\_is\_pointer\_in\_region<!-- {{#callable:memory_layout_is_pointer_in_region}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/kernel/memory_layout.c#L262>)

Checks if a given pointer is within the specified memory region.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``region``: A pointer to an `MpuRegion` structure that defines the memory region to check against.
    - ``ptr``: A pointer to the memory address to check if it is within the specified region.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Convert the `ptr` to an unsigned integer type `uintptr_t` and store it in `p`.
    - Check if `p` is greater than or equal to `region->base_address` and less than `region->base_address + region->size`.
    - Return `true` if both conditions are met, otherwise return `false`.
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the pointer is within the specified memory region, otherwise returns `false`.


---
### memory\_layout\_is\_buffer\_in\_region<!-- {{#callable:memory_layout_is_buffer_in_region}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/kernel/memory_layout.c#L267>)

Checks if a buffer is entirely within a specified memory region.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``region``: A pointer to an `MpuRegion` structure that defines the memory region to check against.
    - ``buf``: A pointer to the start of the buffer to check.
    - ``length``: The length of the buffer in bytes.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`memory_layout_is_pointer_in_region`](<../../../tests/fakes/fake_memory_layout.h.md#memory_layout_is_pointer_in_region>) to check if the start of the buffer (`buf`) is within the `region`.
    - Calls [`memory_layout_is_pointer_in_region`](<../../../tests/fakes/fake_memory_layout.h.md#memory_layout_is_pointer_in_region>) to check if the end of the buffer (`(char *)buf + length - 1`) is within the `region`.
    - Returns `true` if both the start and end of the buffer are within the `region`, otherwise returns `false`.
- **Output**: A boolean value indicating whether the entire buffer is within the specified memory region.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`memory_layout_is_pointer_in_region`](<../../../tests/fakes/fake_memory_layout.h.md#memory_layout_is_pointer_in_region>)


---
### memory\_layout\_is\_cstring\_in\_region<!-- {{#callable:memory_layout_is_cstring_in_region}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/kernel/memory_layout.c#L271>)

Checks if a C-style string is fully contained within a specified memory region and is null-terminated within a given maximum length.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``region``: A pointer to an `MpuRegion` structure that defines the memory region to check.
    - ``str``: A pointer to the C-style string to check.
    - ``max_length``: The maximum length to check for the null-termination of the string.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calculate the end address of the memory region using `region->base_address` and `region->size`.
    - Check if the string pointer `str` is within the bounds of the memory region; if not, return `false`.
    - Determine the maximum end position for the string check using the minimum of the region end and `str + max_length`.
    - Calculate the length of the string using `strnlen` up to the determined maximum end position.
    - Check if the character at the calculated string length is a null character; if not, return `false`.
    - Return `true` if all checks pass, indicating the string is within the region and null-terminated.
- **Output**: A boolean value indicating whether the string is within the specified memory region and null-terminated within the given maximum length.
